description: This false-colour satellite image, credited to the European Union and LUCAS (Land Use/Cover Area frame Survey) from 2012, shows a mountainous Alpine landscape in Austria. A single green dot marker is centrally located in the upper half of the image. The terrain features include ridges and valleys, with diverse colours indicating different land cover types. Dark red areas represent dense vegetation, while lighter green/teal and brown/beige areas suggest sparsely vegetated land or rocky outcrops. This image is relevant for discriminating between land cover categories such as Alpine meadows and mountain hay meadows in high mountain regions.
